title: Kingidium taeniale
taxonomy: Kingidium taeniale is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Kingidium, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. The genus Kingidium is part of the subtribe Aeridinae, which includes a wide range of orchid species. Kingidium taeniale is native to Southeast Asia and is often found growing in humid, shaded environments. This species is highly valued among orchid enthusiasts for its unique flowers and robust growth habit.

intro: Kingidium taeniale, also known as the "Venus Butterfly Orchid", is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its unique, fan-shaped leaves and vibrant, butterfly-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a range of health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties.
